instrument cluster.location: Behind dashboard, center of steering column
instrument cluster.notes: Watch for hidden screws behind dash trim. Label connectors with tape before disconnecting. Premium clusters (Denali, S-Line, M-Sport) have higher margins.
instrument cluster.factory_upgrade: High — Base models often accept premium cluster swaps with coding

ECU.location: Engine bay passenger side firewall OR behind glove box OR under passenger kick panel
ECU.notes: Location varies significantly by make: Honda/Acura = passenger kick panel; Toyota/Lexus = engine bay firewall; GM = engine bay; BMW = under hood on passenger side; VW/Audi = behind glove box or under hood. Check eBay photos for your specific year to confirm.

ABS module.location: Engine bay, mounted to master cylinder/brake booster assembly
ABS module.notes: CRITICAL: Brake fluid will leak. Bring rags. Do NOT cross-thread lines on removal — if line is seized, skip it. Label lines with tape BEFORE disconnecting. The module is the electronic box ON TOP of the valve body, not the whole assembly.

throttle body.location: Intake manifold, top/front of engine — follow big intake hose from air filter
throttle body.notes: Very accessible on most cars. Often has coolant lines attached (pinch clamps). Mark hose orientation if present. Some have 8mm bolts, not 10mm. Hybrid TBs (Prius) are electronically controlled and worth more.

amplifier.location: Trunk/rear cargo area OR under passenger seat OR behind glove box OR under rear deck
amplifier.notes: Bose: often in trunk or rear deck. Harman Kardon: under seat or trunk. Mark Levinson: trunk or rear deck. Burmester/Bowers: trunk. Premium amps have heat sinks = easy ID. Check part number — some cars have multiple amp locations.
amplifier.factory_upgrade: High — Many base cars have wiring for premium audio but no amp. Plug-and-play upgrade for owners.

BCM.location: Under driver dash OR behind glove box OR left of steering column OR under passenger kick panel
BCM.notes: Usually black box with 3-5 large connectors. PHOTO connectors before removal — must go back same way. BCMs are VIN-locked on many newer cars (2010+) and require programming. Check interchange before buying. Some BCMs are integrated with fuse box.

HID ballast.location: Under headlight assembly OR behind bumper cover OR attached to headlight housing OR near frame rail
HID ballast.notes: Small black box ~3x5 inches. Often attached to headlight housing or frame rail. Don't confuse with headlight leveling module (smaller). OEM ballasts (Osram, Philips, Denso, Matsushita) worth more than aftermarket. Both sides usually have separate ballasts.

climate control.location: Center dash behind radio OR integrated with radio bezel
climate control.notes: Often includes blend door actuators. Pull the whole panel as assembly. Dual-zone climate = higher value. Rear climate controls (SUVs/minivans) = separate unit in back, also valuable.
climate control.factory_upgrade: Medium — Some base cars accept dual-zone upgrade with wiring harness swap

fuel pump.location: Top of fuel tank, under rear seat OR trunk floor panel
tools: Fuel line disconnect tool ($5 at parts store)
fuel pump.notes: Tank may be 1/4 full = heavy. Fuel vapors = NO SPARKS/NO SMOKING. Ring retainer may be rusted — bring PB Blaster. Some cars have access panel under seat (easy), others require tank drop (skip unless very valuable).

turbocharger.location: Exhaust manifold side, center of engine bay — look for snail-shaped housing
turbocharger.notes: Rusty exhaust bolts = PB Blaster first. Let sit 10 min. Heavy part — support before final bolt removal. Oil/coolant lines attached — expect drips. Don't lose copper washers on banjo bolts. Check shaft play — grab compressor wheel, should have minimal play.

seat control.location: Under driver seat OR integrated into seat base OR under seat track
seat control.notes: Memory seat cars always have module. Base models may not. Check for heated seat buttons as indicator. Module is small black box with 2-3 connectors. Some integrate into seat motor assembly.
seat control.factory_upgrade: High — Many cars have wiring for memory/heat but no module. Plug-and-play upgrade.

alternator.location: Front of engine, driven by serpentine belt — look for round device with cooling vents
tools: Serpentine belt tool or 3/8 drive ratchet
alternator.notes: Very accessible on most cars. Note belt routing before removal (take photo). The rear bolt is often hard to reach — use extension + swivel. High-output alternators (trucks, police cars, ambulances) = premium.

MAF sensor.location: Intake duct between air filter and throttle body — rectangular plastic housing
tools: MAF cleaner spray (optional for cleaning later)
MAF sensor.notes: Easiest pull on the list. Don't touch sensor wires with fingers — oils damage them. Some use hose clamp, others use 2 screws (Torx). OEM Bosch/Denso sensors worth more than aftermarket.

radio nav.location: Center dash, behind bezel — look for rectangular opening with screen
radio nav.notes: Premium nav units often have separate DVD drive below. Pull both. GPS antenna on dash top. Amplifier may be separate (trunk/under seat). OEM nav units with Apple CarPlay/Android Auto = GOLD. Aftermarket = skip.
radio nav.factory_upgrade: Very High — Base cars accept premium nav with coding. CarPlay/Android Auto units = $500-1000 demand.
